name: Drug Discovery Research Assistant
description: A research co-worker for early drug-discovery triage. Give it a disease or target profile, screening criteria, optional candidate seeds, literature notes, and an input folder for supporting files; it stages an evidence-backed discovery workflow and writes review-ready candidate summaries, scores, assumptions, risks, and source notes to the output folder.

assay_constraints.description: Wet-lab, ADMET, or computational constraints that shape recommendations.

candidate_seed_set.description: Optional seed molecules, protein targets, or prior candidates to compare.

disease_or_target_profile.description: Disease, target, pathway, or phenotype that frames the discovery run.

literature_corpus.description: Research papers, notes, or database exports used as evidence.

screening_criteria.description: Desired candidate, assay, safety, novelty, or evidence criteria.

input_folder.description: Default local input folder for sample files or user-provided files.

output_folder.description: Default local output folder for generated artifacts and reports.

contract.summary: Discovery work is iterative and evidence-heavy; researchers need an assistant that keeps candidate generation, scoring, extraction, and review moving in order.
users: Computational biology researchers, scientific AI platform teams, and research operations groups.

steps.goal: Identify and summarize disease targets and biological constraints.
steps.label: Target Discovery

steps.goal: Generate or retrieve useful target structures for screening.
steps.label: Structure Generation

steps.goal: Generate candidate molecules and filter against screening criteria.
steps.label: Candidate Generation

steps.goal: Evaluate candidate binding, risk, and evidence quality.
steps.label: Binding Evaluation

steps.goal: Rank candidates, summarize evidence, and prepare a human-reviewable research artifact.
steps.label: Ranking & Reporting

rules.message: Human approval is required before this workflow writes or releases high-impact recommendations.

metadata.agent_role: AI assistant for organizing drug discovery candidate generation, filtering, and evidence review.
metadata.customizable_for: Drug discovery, material search, assay planning, scientific literature review, and research pipeline support.
metadata.description: A research co-worker for early drug-discovery triage. Give it a disease or target profile, screening criteria, optional candidate seeds, literature notes, and an input folder for supporting files; it stages an evidence-backed discovery workflow and writes review-ready candidate summaries, scores, assumptions, risks, and source notes to the output folder.

human_control.reason: Human approval is required before high-impact downstream actions such as advance_to_lab_experiment, make_clinical_claim, submit_regulatory_material.

fields.description: Confirm the disease, phenotype, or target family to investigate.

fields.description: Maximum runtime for the staged discovery loop.
fields.label: Runtime limit

init_config_review.instruction: When running in OtterDesk, show these overwriteable fields during init config and ask the user to change them or keep the defaults before launch.

connector.description: Connector-provided target profile and evidence summaries from approved research systems.

file.description: JSON object loaded from a local file path.

json.description: Inline JSON object supplied through config or runtime overrides.

mock.description: Bundled Alzheimer demo target and synthetic candidate workflow.

sample_file.description: JSON disease profile, candidate seeds, and screening criteria loaded from a local file.

artifacts.description: Stable run-store files and domain-specific outputs.

human.description: Human notices, approval requests, responses, timeouts, and applied decisions.

input_skills.description: Connector-backed or reusable skill-backed input sources.

inputs.description: Resolved runtime inputs and adapter provenance.

logs.description: Runtime stdout/stderr and explicit log artifacts.

output_skills.description: Optional fan-out destinations after local run-store writes.

outputs.description: Primary result and final user-facing artifact.

streams.description: Named data or event streams exposed by the blueprint.

metadata.llm_agent_role: AI assistant for organizing drug discovery candidate generation, filtering, and evidence review.
metadata.name: Drug Discovery Research Assistant
metadata.output: Candidate artifacts, stage logs, evidence notes, and discovery summaries for researcher review.

final_artifact.description: The durable user-facing artifact should summarize the result, recommendation, confidence, evidence, next steps, and source references.

output_contract.stdout: single JSON object, event envelope, or bundle path

metadata.problem_solved: Discovery work is iterative and evidence-heavy; researchers need an assistant that keeps candidate generation, scoring, extraction, and review moving in order.

quick_test.description: Use generated quick-test bundles when available; fixed blueprints use local dry-run/mock provider environment variables and deterministic sample inputs.

runtime_features: actor-style LLM specialists use retrieved policy context when available
metadata.simulation_type: Iterative scientific research workflow with staged candidate review.

standard.execution_model: load_metadata -> resolve_config -> load_inputs -> start_run_store -> observe_simulation_state -> call_llm_agent -> evaluate_workflow_state -> route_by_context -> request_human_input -> apply_decision_to_simulation -> emit_events -> write_final_artifact -> fan_out_output_skills

metadata.target_user: Computational biology researchers, scientific AI platform teams, and research operations groups.
metadata.target_users: Computational biology researchers, scientific AI platform teams, and research operations groups.

knowledge_rag.purpose: Retrieve local discovery-triage guidance for target evidence, generated candidate review, docking caveats, and report-only scientific risk checks.
knowledge_rag.grounding_policy: Use retrieved local blueprint knowledge to improve actor review prompts; do not invent facts when retrieval is unavailable.
knowledge_rag.privacy: index local blueprint knowledge only; do not index private run documents unless a future runtime explicitly opts in

agentic_research.note: Scientific workers may use bounded tool outputs and local RAG guidance; failed or thin evidence remains explicit in the review packet.
